headline: Forget Utility Dividends. Kevin Warsh Just Made the 30-Year Treasury a Better Income Play
summary: The bearish case on rate-sensitive regulated utilities at current levels is building, and NextEra Energy (NYSE:NEE) at $95.68 is the cleanest example of what Kevin Warsh’s commitment to quantitative tightening will do to the group. The four other names carrying the same exposure are Dominion Energy (NYSE:D) at $62.97, Eversource Energy (NYSE:ES) at $68.81, Xcel ... Forget Utility Dividends. Kevin Warsh Just Made the 30-Year Treasury a Better Income Play
